cuisine: Contemporary fusion health food (Japanese-Middle Eastern fusion)
region_of_origin: Modern fusion; widely available in Singapore health cafés and salad bars
food_subgroup: Legume-based dip with fresh vegetable sticks (edamame hummus with carrot, cucumber, and celery)

ontology.foodon_label: hummus (closest parent: legume-based dip food product)
ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has hummus (chickpea-based, FOODON_03315595) but no specific entry for edamame hummus. Closest parent is legume-based dip.
ontology.foodex2_description: Legume-based composite dish (closest parent); FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for edamame hummus. A00GG (composite legume dish) used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for edamame hummus exists. A00GG used as closest parent for legume-based composite dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for edamame hummus or edamame-based dip as of 2026.

serving.description: 1 snack portion (80g edamame hummus + 50g carrot + 50g cucumber + 30g celery)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard snack serving is 210g, comprising approximately 80g of edamame hummus with 50g carrot sticks, 50g cucumber sticks, and 30g celery sticks.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Edamame (cooked), Tahini, Carrots (raw), Cucumber (raw), Celery (raw); SR Legacy component analysis
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (ARS), FoodData Central / SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component analysis of individual ingredients (edamame cooked, tahini, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, carrot raw, cucumber raw, celery raw) weighted by recipe proportion for the composite dish (80g hummus portion: ~55% edamame, ~20% tahini, ~10% olive oil, ~10% lemon juice/garlic; 130g raw vegetable sticks). Values represent the blended per-100g composite of the full 210g snack. Cholesterol confirmed zero for all plant-based ingredients. Vitamin B12 confirmed zero for all plant-based ingredients. Vitamin D confirmed zero for plant-based ingredients with no fortification.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Legumes and Composite Legume-Based Dishes — analogous estimate based on edamame (GI ~25) and tahini (GI ~0) weighted composite; reference: University of Sydney GI Database (edamame / soybeans) and Atkinson et al. 2008 (International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for edamame hummus as a composite dish. GI estimated at ~28 using weighted component method: edamame (cooked soybeans) GI ~25 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, soybean entry); tahini GI ~0 (pure sesame paste, negligible available carbohydrate); olive oil GI ~0; raw vegetables (carrot GI ~35, cucumber ~15, celery ~15). Weighted composite across 210g serving yields GI ~28. GL calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (28 × 9.5g × 2.1) / 100 ≈ 5.5. This is a defensible analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: High potassium (258mg/100g; ~542mg per 210g serving), very low sodium (88mg/100g; ~185mg per serving), and edamame isoflavones with mild ACE-inhibitory and endothelial-protective effects
hypertension.guidance: Edamame hummus with vegetable sticks is highly suitable for hypertension management: the favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (~2.9:1 per 100g) supports blood pressure reduction, celery provides phthalides with vasodilatory properties, and soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) have documented mild antihypertensive effects via improved endothelial function.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (~28) and very low glycemic load (~5.5 per 210g serving), high dietary fibre (3.5g/100g), and soy isoflavones with evidence for improved insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Edamame hummus with vegetable sticks is an excellent snack choice for Type 2 Diabetes: the very low GI (~28) and GL (~5.5 per serving) minimise postprandial glucose excursions, the high protein (5.5g/100g) and fibre (3.5g/100g) content promote satiety and slow carbohydrate absorption, and genistein has peer-reviewed evidence for improved insulin sensitivity and reduced postprandial glucose in T2DM.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero cholesterol, very low saturated fat (0.5g/100g), high unsaturated fat from olive oil and tahini, soy isoflavones with LDL-lowering and anti-atherosclerotic properties, and high dietary fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Edamame hummus with vegetable sticks is cardioprotective: zero dietary cholesterol, very low saturated fat (0.5g/100g), beneficial monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats from olive oil and tahini, and soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) have evidence for LDL cholesterol reduction and anti-atherosclerotic effects; the high potassium and fibre content further support cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Edamame isoflavones (genistein ~37mg/100g cooked, daidzein ~23mg/100g) with documented NF-κB inhibition and TNF-alpha/IL-6 reduction; beta-carotene from carrot; folate reducing homocysteine
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Edamame hummus with vegetable sticks is highly suitable for rheumatoid arthritis: genistein inhibits NF-κB and reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6), tahini sesame lignans provide antioxidant anti-inflammatory activity, carrot beta-carotene is an anti-inflammatory carotenoid, and folate (38mcg/100g) helps reduce homocysteine, a cardiovascular risk marker elevated in RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Edamame Hummus with Vegetable Sticks (毛豆泥配蔬菜条; Máodòu Ní Pèi Shūcài Tiáo; Japanese: 枝豆フムスと野菜スティック) is a Japanese-Middle Eastern fusion health snack widely available at Singapore health cafés, salad bars, and supermarkets. It replicates the classic chickpea hummus preparation using edamame (毛豆, immature soybeans harvested before maturity) as the protein-rich legume base, blended with tahini (sesame paste), lemon juice, garlic, and olive oil to produce a smooth, bright-green dip with a sweet, nutty, and slightly earthy flavour. The hummus is served with fresh vegetable sticks — carrot, cucumber, and celery — for dipping. The standard snack portion is 210g (80g edamame hummus + 50g carrot + 50g cucumber + 30g celery). Nutritionally, the composite snack provides 98 kcal/100g, 5.5g protein, 4.8g fat (predominantly unsaturated from olive oil and tahini), and 9.5g carbohydrate with 3.5g dietary fibre. The standout nutritional feature is the isoflavone content: edamame provides genistein (~37mg/100g cooked) and daidzein (~23mg/100g), phytoestrogens with well-documented anti-inflammatory, LDL-lowering, and insulin-sensitising properties. The composite snack has a very low glycemic index (~28) and glycemic load (~5.5 per serving), making it one of the most clinically favourable snack options for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Potassium is high (258mg/100g; ~542mg per serving) and sodium is low (88mg/100g), supporting blood pressure management. Frozen edamame is widely available at NTUC FairPrice, Giant, and Cold Storage in Singapore for approximately SGD 3–5 per 500g bag, making this snack accessible and easy to prepare at home.
common_user_queries: is edamame hummus good for diabetes

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component analysis of individual ingredients weighted by recipe proportion for the composite dish. No single approved-source entry exists for edamame hummus as a composite dish. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database soybean/edamame entry and Atkinson et al. 2008 weighted composite method. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact dish is not directly measured in any approved primary source.
notes: 1. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — all ingredients are plant-based with no animal-derived components; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for edamame, tahini, olive oil, and raw vegetables. 2. Vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — this is an entirely plant-based food with no animal products or fortification; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central. 3. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — no animal-derived ingredients and no fortification; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central. 4. Trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — no hydrogenated oils used; olive oil and tahini are naturally trans-fat-free per USDA FoodData Central. 5. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite blended dish (edamame hummus + raw vegetable sticks) is not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central as a single composite entry; individual ingredient moisture values vary significantly (edamame cooked ~67% water, cucumber raw ~95% water, celery raw ~95% water, tahini ~3% water) and a reliable blended composite figure cannot be derived without direct measurement of the prepared dish. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy was consulted but does not provide a composite moisture value for this specific preparation. 6. Monounsaturated_fat_g (2.1g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.4g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles for olive oil and tahini weighted by recipe proportion; these are approximate values. 7. FoodEx2 code A00GG used as closest parent for legume-based composite dish — no exact code exists for edamame hummus. 8. GI value of 28 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement of edamame hummus as a composite dish is recommended for clinical applications. 9. Vitamin A reported as mcg RAE (45.0 mcg RAE/100g), primarily from beta-carotene in carrot sticks. 10. Folate reported as mcg DFE (38.0 mcg DFE/100g), primarily from edamame.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Added Japanese local name '枝豆フムスと野菜スティック' to local_names and aliases_by_language to address G6 and Q1 multilingual compliance for Japanese-origin ingredient (edamame). Added '枝豆フムスと野菜スティック' to flat aliases array for Q8 consistency.

Iteration 3: Expanded water_g = -1 documentation in notes field to explicitly state: (a) the specific field set to -1, (b) the reason data is unavailable (composite dish moisture not reported as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; individual ingredient moisture values vary significantly and cannot be reliably blended without direct measurement), and (c) the source consulted that confirmed unavailability (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy). This resolves the G3 numeric hygiene failure from iteration 2 validator feedback.
